name: HTX (formerly Huobi) Cryptocurrency Exchange API
what_it_does: Major cryptocurrency exchange REST and WebSocket API (formerly Huobi Global, rebranded HTX 2023) — enabling traders and algorithmic trading systems to access spot, margin, futures, options, and swap markets across 600+ trading pairs, with HMAC-SHA256 authentication, comprehensive REST API for order management, WebSocket for real-time streaming, and official SDKs in multiple languages.
use_cases: Executing algorithmic spot and margin trades via HTX REST API for automated strategies
Streaming real-time order book and ticker data via HTX WebSocket for market analysis
Managing futures and swap positions via HTX Derivatives API for hedging automation
Accessing wide trading pair selection via HTX API for altcoin diversification strategies
Migrating from Huobi to HTX using same API endpoints (same underlying platform)
not_for: US users (HTX/Huobi does not serve US customers — use Coinbase or Kraken)
Institutional custody with regulated reporting (use regulated venues for compliance)
Teams needing CFTC/SEC compliant derivatives (HTX derivatives not available to US residents)
best_when: An Asian or international trader needs a large exchange with wide pair selection and deep liquidity for spot, margin, and derivatives across major and altcoin markets outside the US.
avoid_when: You're in the US, need regulatory compliance, or require institutional custody services.

auth.notes: API Key + API Secret for HMAC-SHA256 signing. Signature includes: AccessKeyId, SignatureMethod (HmacSHA256), SignatureVersion (2), Timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC). GET: parameters sorted + encoded in canonical query string. POST: parameters in request body (separate signature). Signature in URL query params (not header). API key permissions: Read, Trade, Withdraw. No OAuth2. WebSocket auth via signing authentication message.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; maker 0.20% / taker 0.20% (HT token discounts available)
pricing.paid_tiers: VIP tiers based on 30-day volume with reduced fees
pricing.notes: Founded 2013 in China (Leon Li). One of the largest exchanges by volume. Relocated from China to Seychelles after China crypto ban 2021. Rebranded from Huobi Global to HTX in September 2023. HTX stands for 'Huobi Tech eXchange' (maintaining brand heritage). Justin Sun (TRON founder) became strategic advisor/investor 2022 — controversial. HT Token for fee discounts. 600+ trading pairs. Deep liquidity on BTC/ETH pairs. Spot, margin, futures (coin-margined and USDT-margined), options. Official SDKs in 6 languages. Chinese developer community large — documentation quality variable. API documentation still partially references 'Huobi'.

performance.rate_limits: 1,200 requests per minute for spot; varies by endpoint category

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HMAC-SHA256 with ISO 8601 timestamp. Signature in URL params (not header). Rebranded Huobi → HTX 2023. Justin Sun involvement controversy. US blocked. Seychelles. Documentation still references Huobi in places. 6 official SDKs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order reads idempotent by order ID. client-order-id for custom deduplication. Cancel idempotent by order ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Rebranded: Huobi Global → HTX in September 2023 — documentation may use both names
Signature in URL query string (not Authorization header) — different from most exchanges
Timestamp format: ISO 8601 UTC (e.g., 2024-01-15T10:00:00 — NOT Unix timestamp)
GET vs POST: different signature construction — GET params sorted in canonical form
US users blocked — verify jurisdiction before building HTX integrations
Justin Sun strategic advisor role since 2022 — vendor risk assessment recommended

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 signing with ISO 8601 timestamp. API key permission scopes. Seychelles jurisdiction (limited regulatory oversight). US blocked. Justin Sun investor (SEC fraud allegations against Sun personally). Cryptocurrency financial data — high value target. No OAuth2. Signature in URL (less secure than header — avoid logging URLs).
